theme: Five rules for incentive trips that work

linkedin.text: Five things that separate an incentive trip people love from one they tolerate: 1) Reward, don't schedule — free time is the gift, not the filler. 2) One unforgettable hero moment beats five good ones. 3) Mix the org chart at dinner; that's where culture is built. 4) Handle every transfer invisibly — friction is the only thing guests remember as 'bad'. 5) Brief, don't manage — your people are adults on holiday. We build all five in from day one. From business to pleasure. Want our full planning checklist? DM us. #UproductionEvents #IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #EventTips #EventPlanning #MICE #B2B #TeamCulture
linkedin.cta: DM us or book a call

image_prompt: Overhead aerial of hundreds of attendees in motion across a grand open plaza at midday, tiny figures from above casting long soft shadows, organized into loose clusters near welcome stations and shaded lounges, lush landscaping framing the space, the geometry of the crowd flow clearly visible from height. Photorealistic, 35mm film grain, no CGI, no text, no logos.
